heading: Printing reports generally
label: 556 Printing reports generally
text: The Secretary of the Army shall cause the manuscript of the annual report of the Chief of Engineers and subordinate engineers, relating to the improvement of rivers and harbors, and the report of the Mississippi River Commission to be placed in the hands of the Director of the Government Publishing Office on or before the 15th day of October in each year, and the Director of the Government Publishing Office shall cause said reports to be printed with an accurate and comprehensive index thereof, on or before the first Monday in December in each year, for the use of Congress.All reports on examinations and surveys which may be prepared during the recess of Congress shall, in the discretion of the Secretary of the Army, be printed by the Director of the Government Publishing Office as documents of the following session of Congress.
notes: CodificationThe first paragraph of this section is from act , popularly known as the “Rivers and Harbors Appropriation Act”.Aug. 11, 1888The Mississippi River Commission was created by act , set out as sections 641, 642, 644, 646, and 647 of this title.June 28, 1879The words “and Missouri” which appeared in the original text after “Mississippi” were superseded by the abolition of the Missouri River Commission by , .act June 13, 1902, ch. 1079, § 132 Stat. 367The second paragraph of this section is from the last paragraph of section 3 of act , which superseded a similar provision of , . Other parts of said section 3 are set out in .Mar. 4, 1913act July 25, 1912, ch. 25337 Stat. 231section 545 of this title

Change of Name“Director of the Government Publishing Office” substituted for “Public Printer” in text on authority of , set out as a note under , Public Printing and Documents.section 1301(d) of Pub. L. 113–235section 301 of Title 44Department of War designated Department of the Army and title of Secretary of War changed to Secretary of the Army by section 205(a) of , . Section 205(a) of act , was repealed by section 53 of , . Section 1 of act , enacted “Title 10, Armed Forces” which in sections 3010 to 3013 continued Department of the Army under administrative supervision of Secretary of the Army.act July 26, 1947, ch. 343, title II61 Stat. 501act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 641July 26, 1947Aug. 10, 1956
